obligation_text: Before deploying a high-risk AI system, deployers that are bodies governed by public law, or are private entities providing public services, and deployers of high-risk AI systems referred to in points 5(b) and 5(c) of Annex III shall perform an assessment of the impact on fundamental rights that the use of such system may produce.
scoring_text: Skill in conducting fundamental rights impact assessments for AI systems, including methodology selection, fundamental rights identification, impact evaluation, and assessment documentation for both public and private entities.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in conducting fundamental rights impact assessments for AI systems, including methodology selection, fundamental rights identification, impact evaluation, and assessment documentation for both public and private entities.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identification, bias evaluation, human oversight documentation, and residual risk determination.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 27(1) (obligation) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-005 is the strongest match (STS: 0.645, cosine: 0.679). Secondary: AG-K-004 (AG, cosine: 0.647), AG-T-010 (AG, cosine: 0.617).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.5/10 (raw 0.645). Shared vocabulary: [ai, assessments, conducting, documentation, evaluation, identification, impact, skill]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [assessment, entities, fundamental, methodology, private, public] vs WIDAI terms [bias, determination, harm, human, oversight, residual] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.914 (P=0.914, R=0.915). Balanced P/R — the 27-word competency implication and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (98.3%). Semantic tension between "Skill in conducting fundamental rights impact assessments for AI systems, includ" and "Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identifi". Despite STS-based 'Superset of' classification, these concepts may operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.679 ≈ STS 0.645 (gap 0.034). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, assessments, conducting, documentation, evaluation] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 36.7%. Shared tokens: [ai, assessments, conducting, documentation, evaluation, identification, impact, skill]. EU AI Act-only: [assessment, entities, fundamental, methodology, private, public]. WIDAI-only: [bias, determination, harm, human, oversight, residual].
